text.title: Circuit Breaker and Resilience
text.summary: Circuit breaker implementation with CLOSED/OPEN/HALF_OPEN states and resilient HTTP client wrapper.

rule.statement: Every HTTP call to another service must go through a circuit breaker. When the failure threshold is exceeded, the breaker opens and fails fast instead of waiting for timeout.
rule.rationale: Without a circuit breaker, a slow or unavailable downstream service holds connections and threads in the caller, cascading into resource exhaustion across the entire system.

rule.statement: Circuit breaker states: CLOSED (normal), OPEN (fail fast, reset after recovery_timeout), HALF_OPEN (test with limited calls, close if successful).
rule.rationale: HALF_OPEN prevents the breaker from staying open indefinitely after the downstream service recovers. It allows gradual traffic restoration.
